meta.title: Café Luna · roastery & brunch · Prague
meta.description: A small roastery, brunch bistro and natural-wine bar in Vinohrady, Prague. Specialty coffee, weekly single-origin and a short seasonal menu.

hero.title: Coffee for slow mornings
hero.sub: Café Luna is a small roastery and brunch bistro on Vinohradská 14. We roast every Tuesday, serve brunch every weekend and pour natural wine on Friday evenings.

s1.name: Cardamom flat white
s1.desc: Our morning roast pulled long, with a hint of cardamom and silky-steamed oat milk.

s2.name: Spring shakshuka
s2.desc: Two poached eggs in a green-tomato and mint sauce, with feta from Polabí and a thick slice of sourdough.

s3.name: Rhubarb & vanilla galette
s3.desc: Buttery shortcrust, rhubarb from Mělník and a crackling layer of demerara sugar on top.

origin.title: Yirgacheffe, Ethiopia
origin.sub: Roasted in-house last Tuesday. Available on filter and as espresso while it lasts.

menu.label: The menu
menu.title: Honest food, seasonal drinks
menu.lead: A short, seasonal menu. Coffee from our own roastery, brunch all weekend and natural wine from 5 pm on Fridays.
menu.footer: All prices in CZK · vegetarian unless noted · we list allergens on request.

i1.name: Sourdough & eggs
i1.desc: Two poached eggs, smoked salt and herb butter

i2.name: Spring shakshuka
i2.desc: Green tomato and mint, feta and sourdough

i3.name: Avocado on rye
i3.desc: Smashed avocado, chilli oil, lime and dukkah

i4.name: Granola bowl
i4.desc: House granola, yoghurt, seasonal fruit and honey

i5.name: Salmon plate
i5.desc: Cured salmon, dill cream, capers and rye

i6.name: Pancake stack
i6.desc: Buttermilk pancakes, maple syrup and seasonal compote

i3.name: Cinnamon scroll
i3.desc: Cardamom and cinnamon — a Saturday tradition

i6.name: Choc-tahini cookie
i6.desc: Dark chocolate, sea salt and sesame

src.title: Coffee with a name attached
src.p1: We work with three importers who buy directly from farms in Ethiopia, Kenya and Colombia. Every bag has the grower's name — we know who picked the cherries and pay a fair price for their work.
src.p2: Our brunch comes from Polabí, Mělník and Naše farma in Líbeznice. The bakery is daily, the menu is short and there's almost nothing left over.
src.pull: "Slow coffee, honest food and the same baristas every morning."

t1.quote: I work from Luna three mornings a week. The coffee is the best in Vinohrady, the staff know my order — and the rhubarb galette is dangerous.
t1.name: Marek

t2.quote: Brunch every Saturday, no exceptions. Their shakshuka has ruined every other version for me.
t2.name: Petra

t3.quote: Friday natural-wine night is the best small ritual in Praha 2 right now. A short list with a lot of personality.
t3.name: Ondřej

t4.quote: You can taste that they actually care — about the coffee, the bread, even the playlist.
t4.name: Lucia

news.title: A short letter, once a month
news.text: What we're roasting, who's pouring, what's on the brunch board. Two minutes to read and never any spam.

visit.label: Visit us
visit.title: Vinohradská 14, Prague 2
visit.text: 100 metres from Náměstí Míru — look for the green awning. Walk-ins are welcome on weekdays; for weekend brunch we'd recommend booking ahead.

cookies.text: We use cookies to give you a better experience.

menu_pg.meta_desc: Our complete menu — coffee from our roastery, weekend brunch, daily bakery, natural wine and a short pantry list to take home. Updated every Monday.
menu_pg.title: Honest food, seasonal drinks
menu_pg.lead: A short, deliberately small list. The brunch board changes on Mondays, the bakery rotates daily and our natural-wine list is whatever the grower just delivered.

coffee.title: Coffee · our own roastery
coffee.note: — roasted on Tuesday, served all week —

r1.name: Espresso
r1.desc: House roast, ristretto by default. Single shot.

r2.name: Long black
r2.desc: Double shot poured over hot water.

r3.name: Flat white
r3.desc: Whole, oat, almond or soy milk — same price.

r5.name: V60 filter
r5.desc: Today's single origin — ask the barista about its tasting notes.

r7.name: Cardamom flat white
r7.desc: Our house roast with oat milk and a hint of cardamom.

r8.name: Affogato
r8.desc: Vanilla gelato with a double espresso. Weekends only.

r9.name: Hot chocolate
r9.desc: 70 % dark, oat milk and a pinch of sea salt.

r11.name: House chai
r11.desc: Cardamom, clove and ginger. Brewed properly, never from syrup.

r1.name: Sourdough & eggs
r1.desc: Two poached eggs on toasted Antonínka loaf, smoked salt, a quenelle of herb butter and soft greens.

r2.name: Spring shakshuka
r2.desc: Two poached eggs in a green-tomato and mint sauce, feta from Polabí and Antonínka sourdough on the side.

r3.name: Avocado on rye
r3.desc: Smashed avocado, chilli oil, lime and dukkah on Šumava rye. Add a poached egg for +35.

r4.name: Granola bowl
r4.desc: House granola, set yoghurt, seasonal fruit compote and raw honey. Vegan with coconut yoghurt.

r5.name: Salmon plate
r5.desc: House-cured salmon, dill cream, capers, pickled shallot and rye toasts.

r6.name: Buttermilk pancakes
r6.desc: A stack of three with maple syrup and a seasonal compote (today: rhubarb and vanilla).

r7.name: Slow-cooked beans
r7.desc: Borlotti beans cooked overnight in tomato and smoked paprika, a Polabí egg on top, sourdough.

r8.name: Brunch board (for two)
r8.desc: Sourdough, salmon, soft cheese, pickles, two soft-boiled eggs, jam, honey and two coffees.

r2.name: Quiche of the day
r2.desc: Today: leek and gruyère. Side salad included.

r3.name: Open-face sandwich
r3.desc: On Antonínka sourdough — choose two: salmon, soft cheese, ham or mushroom paté.

r4.name: Grain & green bowl
r4.desc: Pearl barley, lemon-roasted root veg, dukkah and herb yoghurt (or vegan tahini).

r5.name: Chicken plate
r5.desc: Free-range chicken, herby couscous, harissa yoghurt and pickled cabbage.

r6.name: Salad of the day
r6.desc: Whatever's best at the market — just ask the kitchen.

r4.name: Cinnamon scroll
r4.desc: Cardamom and cinnamon — a Saturday tradition.

r7.name: Choc-tahini cookie
r7.desc: Dark chocolate, sea salt and sesame.

r8.name: Sourdough loaf
r8.desc: 800 g loaf to take home, baked daily until sold out.

r3.name: Frankovka
r3.desc: South Moravia, light and juicy · 0,75 l.

r5.name: Cheese plate
r5.desc: Three cheeses with Polabí honey, walnuts and sourdough.

r6.name: Olives & bread
r6.desc: Castelvetrano olives, sourdough and olive oil.

r7.name: Charcuterie board
r7.desc: Three meats, two pickles, cornichons and sourdough.

r8.name: Anchovy toast
r8.desc: Cantabrian anchovies, butter, lemon zest and sourdough.

r1.name: House beans · 250 g
r1.desc: Today's single origin, whole or ground.

r2.name: House granola · 400 g
r2.desc: Oats, almonds, hazelnuts, maple syrup and sea salt.

r3.name: Sourdough starter
r3.desc: Live, in a jar, with care instructions.

r4.name: Polabí honey · 250 g
r4.desc: From a small apiary outside Mělník.

r6.name: Coffee subscription
r6.desc: 250 g every two weeks · pause any time.

key.label: Diet & allergen key
key.foot: A full allergen list is available — please tell your barista about any allergies before ordering.

ev_pg.meta_desc: Friday natural-wine evenings, monthly coffee cuppings, supper clubs and the occasional book launch. Here's what's coming up at Café Luna.
ev_pg.title: A small calendar
ev_pg.lead: Friday natural-wine evenings every week, a monthly coffee cupping and the occasional supper club. Most events are free or pay-what-you-want — a few are ticketed.

hl.title: Spring Supper Club with chef Eliška Procházková
hl.text: A six-course tasting menu built around the first Polabí asparagus and Mělník rhubarb. Eliška takes over our pass for one evening only — twenty seats, served family-style at the long table.

e1.title: Friday wine night · Slovenian skin-contact
e1.desc: Six bottles open at the bar with olives, sourdough and anchovy toasts. No tickets — walk in or reserve a table. Closing at 23:00.

e2.title: Monthly coffee cupping · Ethiopia focus
e2.desc: Six lots from Yirgacheffe, Sidamo and Guji, blind-tasted on the cupping table. Hosted by our roaster Tomáš.

e3.title: Friday wine night · Czech pet-nat showcase
e3.desc: Five bottles from three Moravian growers. The bar opens by the glass at 5 pm and full bottles from 6 pm.

e4.title: Spring Supper Club with Eliška Procházková
e4.desc: Six-course family-style tasting. First asparagus, Mělník rhubarb, slow-cooked lamb and fresh cheeses. Twenty seats only.

e5.title: Sourdough workshop with Antonínka bakery
e5.desc: Three hours, hands-on. Take home a starter and your first loaf. Coffee and brunch are included between proves.

e6.title: Slow Saturday · solo guitar with Jan Veselý
e6.desc: A quiet acoustic set during brunch. No cover charge — just stay longer than you'd planned.

e7.title: Book launch · "Slow Coffee" with Anna Kubíčková
e7.desc: A reading from Anna's first book on specialty-coffee culture in central Europe, with a Q&A and a glass of pet-nat.

e8.title: Brewing at home · pour-over essentials
e8.desc: Two hours with our head barista on grind, ratio and the only three things that actually matter at home. Take home a 250 g bag of today's filter coffee.

e9.title: Closed for a private event · Studio Luna anniversary
e9.desc: The café is closed all day for a private booking. The bar reopens on Friday at 8 am — apologies for the inconvenience.

e10.title: Friday wine night · Italian orange wine
e10.desc: Five bottles from Friuli and Sicily. A light supper menu in the kitchen and a live DJ from 8 pm.

ev_pg.foot: Want to host a private event for 12+ people? Email ahoj@cafeluna.cz with a date and a sentence about the occasion.

visit_pg.meta_desc: Reserve a table at Café Luna in Vinohrady, Prague 2. Brunch, weeknight bistro, Friday wine night, private hire. We confirm by SMS within an hour.
visit_pg.title: Reserve a table
visit_pg.lead: Walk-ins welcome. For weekend brunch, Friday wine night or groups of 6+, we recommend reserving. We confirm by SMS within an hour.

f.notes_ph: A birthday cake, allergies, prefer the long table, dog under it…

aside.title: A few useful notes
aside.l1: Walk-ins are always welcome on weekdays — we hold roughly half the room for the morning crowd.
aside.l2: For tables of six or more, please reserve at least 24 hours ahead — we'll set up the long table specially.
aside.l3: Brunch reservations run for 90 minutes. We'll never rush your coffee — we just won't seat the same chair twice.
aside.l4: Dogs are welcome — there's a water bowl by the door and a treat behind the bar.
aside.l5: Two prams fit comfortably; with three or more, please pick a quieter time slot.
aside.l6: Working from Luna is fine until 11 am on weekdays. Weekends and evenings after 5 pm are screen-free.

gt.label: Getting here
gt.title: Vinohradská 14, Prague 2
gt.lead: A 2-minute walk from Náměstí Míru (line A) or 6 minutes from Muzeum (lines A and C). Tram 11 stops directly in front of the door.
gt.metro_d: Náměstí Míru (A) · 2-min walk · turn left out of the station.
gt.tram_d: Trams 11 and 13 stop right in front (Italská). Night tram 91 stops here too.
gt.bike_d: Two racks under the awning. The nearest Rekola station is 60 m east on Vinohradská.
